sections.paragraph:
ἀρχιλῃστής, οῦ, ὁ (Herodian Gr. I 82, 26; Ps.-Callisth. 1, 36; PCairMasp I, 2 III, 22 [Byz.]; Jos., Bell. 1, 204, V 105; loanw. in rabb.) robber chieftain s. Tdf. on the Syriac trad. of J 18:40 (cp. the use of ἀ. passim in the Byzantine story, ed. JWortley, De Latrone Converso, The Tale of the Converted Robber: Byzantion 66, ’96, 219–43 [text 235–37; first published AnalBoll 100, ’82, 351–63]).—DELG s.v. λεία (on λῃστής).

Herodian Herodian = Herodianus Historicus, III a.d.—List 5

Ps.-Callisth Ps.-Callisth , Historia Alexandri Magni, II–III? a.d.—Lists 5

PCairMasp PCairMasp = JMaspero, Papyrus grecs d’époque byzantine—List 4

Byz. Byz. = Byzantine, esp. in ref. to papyri

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

loanw. loanw. = loanword

rabb. rabb. = rabbinical (literature etc.)

Tdf. Tdf. = CvTischendorf—List 1, beg.

trad. trad. = tradition

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts
  2. ed. = edited (by), edition

Byzantion Byzantion , revue internationale—List 6

AnalBoll AnalBoll = Analecta Bollandiana—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 139
